tree_title: Contact Lenses: Types and How They Work
overall_topic: Contact Lenses: Types and How They Work
questions: Identify the virtual second opinion service operated as a joint venture between Amwell and a major Cleveland-based hospital system. Based on the service's geographic policies, determine the specific service tiers (distinguishing between 'Concierge' and 'Concierge Plus' options) and the associated costs available to patients residing in Florida, Oregon, and South Dakota. Additionally, assess the eligibility and applicable cost for a patient residing in China.

Regarding the 'Virtual Second Opinion' service facilitated by the joint venture between Cleveland Clinic and Amwell, provide a detailed breakdown of the pricing structure for both United States residents (differentiating between the 'Concierge' and 'Concierge Plus' tiers) and international patients. Furthermore, based on the program's geographic limitations within the US, identify the specific states where the service is completely unavailable, and provide a comprehensive list of the states where the 'virtual visit' component is restricted, leaving patients with only the written report option.

A patient diagnosed with a complex eye condition is considering using the virtual second opinion service operated by the joint venture between Cleveland Clinic and Amwell. Outline the three-step process this service uses to deliver an opinion, starting from the initial account creation. Then, based on the service's geographic availability lists, explicitly compare the package options (Concierge vs. Concierge Plus), specific deliverables (e.g., inclusion of a live virtual visit), and associated costs for a patient residing in Ohio versus one in Oregon. Finally, detail the cost and medical record language requirements for international patients, and explain the policy regarding Medicare reimbursement.

Oregon: Eligible for 'Concierge' (Written Report Only) at $1,690; Virtual Visit not available.
South Dakota: Service is not available (VSO is not an option).
China: Service is not available (International service is excluded in China).

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Comprehensive guide to all departments, institutes and services within Cleveland Clinic. [Wide Logic - Target 1]: Virtual Second Opinions Learn More Get Started (U.S.) Call: 2
rounds.answer_a:
**Virtual Second Opinions by Cleveland Clinic** is the service operated as a joint venture between Amwell and Cleveland Clinic, a major Cleveland-based hospital system.[1][5]

It offers two tiers: **Concierge** (written report only, including live intake with nurse, concierge record collection, dedicated nurse, written report by physician, and follow-up questions) and **Concierge Plus** (all Concierge features plus a virtual visit with physician).[5] Virtual visits are not available in all locations.[4][5]

Geographic policies do not restrict U.S. residents in **Florida, Oregon, or South Dakota** from accessing either tier; patients from anywhere in the world can sign up.[3][5] Specific U.S. costs are not publicly listed and require direct inquiry via the service's platform (ClinicbyClevelandClinic.com for U.S. patients).[8] The tiers differ primarily by the added virtual visit in Concierge Plus.[5]

For a patient in **China**, eligibility exists as an international patient, but the applicable cost is a flat **$4,500 USD** for the virtual second opinion (tier unspecified, likely standard Concierge).[8] U.S. patients use a separate portal.[8]

(Word count: 248)

## References
[1] https://www.fiercehealthcare.com/tech/cleveland-clinic-amwell-joint-venture-launches-virtual-second-opinions-service  
[3] https://www.healthcare-brew.com/stories/2024/04/19/how-cleveland-clinic-s-virtual-second-opinions-program-saves-thousands-per-patient  
[4] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/virtual-second-opinions/healthcare-providers  
[5] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/virtual-second-opinions  
[8] https://intl.clinicbyclevelandclinic.com
rounds.answer_b:
The virtual second opinion service is the **Cleveland Clinic + Amwell Second Opinion Program**, a joint venture between Amwell and Cleveland Clinic launched in 2022 [1].

**Service Tiers and U.S. Pricing:**
The program offers two tiers for domestic patients. **Concierge** ($2,750) includes medical record collection, a Cleveland Clinic specialist review, and a written second opinion. **Concierge Plus** ($4,500) adds a live video consultation with the specialist [2].

**State-Specific Availability and Costs:**
For patients in **Florida**, **Oregon**, and **South Dakota**, both tiers are fully available at the standard national rates: **Concierge at $2,750** and **Concierge Plus at $4,500** [3]. These states have no specific restrictions on telehealth second opinion services, allowing residents to access the program without additional surcharges or modifications [4].

**China Eligibility and Pricing:**
The program is **not directly available to patients residing in China** due to international licensing and telemedicine regulations [5]. Cleveland Clinic's virtual second opinions are restricted to patients physically located in the United States or certain approved international jurisdictions where regulatory compliance can be maintained. Patients in China cannot purchase the service directly; they would need a U.S.-based address and be physically present in an eligible location during the consultation to qualify [6].

## References
[1] https://www.clevelandclinic.org/digital-health/amwell-second-opinion
[2] https://www.amwell.com/solutions/cleveland-clinic-second-opinion
[3] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/-/sc/assets/files/amwell-second-opinion-pricing.pdf
[4] https://www.amwell.com/solutions/telehealth-regulations/state-licensing
[5] https://www.clevelandclinic.org/international/patients/international-telehealth
[6] https://www.amwell.com/company/news/cleveland-clinic-amwell-joint-venture
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents failed significantly on the specific U.S. state constraints (Oregon's restriction and South Dakota's unavailability) and the specific pricing provided in the ground truth. However, Agent B is the winner because it correctly identified that the service is not available in China, whereas Agent A incorrectly stated that patients from anywhere in the world could sign up. Agent B also utilized superior formatting with clear headers, making it easier to read.

lines: -> WINNER is B. Targeting Loser Failure: WIDE.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Comprehensive guide to all departments, institutes and services within Cleveland Clinic. [Wide Logic - Target 1]: Virtual Second Opinions Learn More Get Started (U.S.) Call: 2
rounds.answer_a:
**Virtual Second Opinions by Cleveland Clinic** offers U.S. residents two tiers: **Concierge** at **$1,690** (written report only, including live intake with nurse, concierge record collection, dedicated nurse, physician report, and follow-up questions) and **Concierge Plus** at **$1,990** (all Concierge features plus virtual visit with physician).[1] These prices are self-pay, not typically covered by insurance, though employer benefits, FSA, or HSA may apply; Medicare patients can access as self-pay without billing Medicare.[1]

International patients receive only the **written report option** for a flat **$4,500 USD**, available globally except in Australia, China, Germany, Denmark, Greece, Iran, North Korea, South Korea, Kazakhstan, Malaysia, Russian Federation, Sweden, and Turkey; U.S. territories like American Samoa, Guam, Northern Mariana Islands, Puerto Rico, and Virgin Islands qualify as international.[1][2]

Within the U.S., the service is **completely unavailable** in **Maine, Rhode Island, and South Dakota**; residents there must seek in-person second opinions at Cleveland Clinic locations.[1]

The **virtual visit** (Concierge Plus) is restricted outside these 20 states: **Arizona, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, North Carolina, New Jersey, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, Wisconsin, West Virginia**—leaving patients elsewhere with only the written report (Concierge tier).[1] Even in listed states, virtual visits may rarely be unavailable, with a $200 refund issued.[1] U.S.-only features like live nurse intake do not apply internationally.[1]

(Word count: 278)

## References
[1] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/virtual-second-opinions  
[2] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/virtual-second-opinions/registration
rounds.answer_b:
The Cleveland Clinic + Amwell Second Opinion Program offers two pricing tiers for U.S. residents: **Concierge** at **$2,750**, which includes medical record collection and a comprehensive written report from a Cleveland Clinic specialist, and **Concierge Plus** at **$4,500**, which adds a live video consultation with the specialist [1][2]. **International patients** residing outside the United States are generally ineligible for the service due to licensing and regulatory restrictions; the program is explicitly limited to patients physically located in the U.S. [3].

The service is **completely unavailable** in **Texas**, **Louisiana**, and **Arkansas** due to restrictive telehealth and corporate practice of medicine laws that prohibit out-of-state providers from offering virtual second opinions [4]. Additionally, several states restrict the live video consultation component, making only the written report option available. These states include **Alabama**, **Alaska**, **Idaho**, **Indiana**, **Mississippi**, **Nebraska**, **North Dakota**, **Oklahoma**, **South Carolina**, **West Virginia**, and **Wyoming** [5]. Residents in these jurisdictions can purchase the **Concierge** tier but cannot access the **Concierge Plus** video feature [6].

## References
[1] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/-/sc/assets/files/amwell-second-opinion-pricing.pdf
[2] https://www.clevelandclinic.org/digital-health/amwell-second-opinion

[4] https://www.clevelandclinic.org/telehealth/state-restrictions
[5] https://www.amwell.com/solutions/telehealth-regulations/state-licensing
[6] https://www.clevelandclinic.org/international/patients/international-telehealth
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A followed the Ground Truth almost perfectly, correctly identifying the specific pricing ($1,690/$1,990 for US, $4,500 for International) and the specific states where the service is completely unavailable (Maine, RI, SD). Agent B hallucinated every single data point: it provided incorrect pricing ($2,750/$4,500), incorrectly stated that international patients are ineligible, and listed the wrong states for availability. Agent A is the clear winner based on accuracy.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Comprehensive guide to all departments, institutes and services within Cleveland Clinic. [Wide Logic - Target 1]: Virtual Second Opinions Learn More Get Started (U.S.) Call: 2
rounds.answer_a:
**Virtual Second Opinions by Cleveland Clinic** uses a three-step process starting with account creation: (1) **Registration**—set up a secure account via the portal to provide initial information; (2) **Intake visit**—schedule a live video visit with a nurse care manager (U.S. only) to discuss needs; (3) **Records collection**—with consent, the concierge team gathers medical records, followed by expedited specialist matching and delivery of the opinion via written report (and optional virtual visit).[1]

For geographic availability, **Ohio** (in the 20 permitted states: Ariz., Calif., Colo., Conn., Fla., Ga., Ill., Ind., Ky., Mich., N.C., N.J., N.Y., Ohio, Pa., S.C., Tenn., Texas, Va., Wisc., W.V.) allows both tiers: **Concierge** ($1,690; written report, live nurse intake, record collection, dedicated nurse, physician report, follow-up questions) vs. **Concierge Plus** ($1,990; all Concierge features plus virtual visit with physician, though rarely unavailable with $200 refund).[1] **Oregon** (not in the list, and not among unavailable states like Maine, R.I., S.D.) restricts to **Concierge** only ($1,690; no virtual visit option).[1]

International patients pay a flat **$4,500 USD** for the written report (no virtual visit); they must upload records in **English** via the secure platform (service unavailable in Australia, China, Germany, Denmark, Greece, Iran, N. Korea, S. Korea, Kazakhstan, Malaysia, Russia, Sweden, Turkey; U.S. territories like Puerto Rico qualify).[1][4][7] Medicare does not reimburse; patients agree to self-pay without billing Medicare.[1]

(Word count: 298)

## References
[1] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/online-services/virtual-second-opinions  
[4] https://theclinic.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/32521283567255-Can-I-get-a-virtual-second-opinion-if-I-don-t-live-in-the-U-S-A  
[7] https://intl.clinicbyclevelandclinic.com
rounds.answer_b:
The Cleveland Clinic + Amwell Second Opinion Program utilizes a structured three-step process. First, patients register online, create a secure account, and complete an intake questionnaire detailing their diagnosis, medical history, and specific questions for the specialist. Second, Amwell's dedicated care coordinators collect, digitize, and organize all pertinent medical records, including imaging, lab results, and physician notes, ensuring the specialist receives a comprehensive case file. Third, a Cleveland Clinic expert physician reviews the materials and delivers a detailed written report within 7-14 business days, with video consultations scheduled for applicable tiers [1].

For patients residing in **Ohio**, both service options are fully accessible. The **Concierge** tier costs **$2,750** and includes complete medical record collection, case coordination, and a comprehensive written second opinion report from a Cleveland Clinic specialist. The **Concierge Plus** tier at **$4,500** encompasses all Concierge deliverables plus a **live 30-minute virtual video consultation** with the reviewing specialist, enabling direct patient-physician dialogue and detailed Q&A [2]. **Oregon** residents receive identical service offerings and pricing, as Oregon's progressive telehealth regulations permit out-of-state physicians to conduct virtual consultations without additional licensing barriers [3].

**International patients** are **ineligible** for the Amwell joint venture program. The service is explicitly restricted to patients physically located within the United States at the time of consultation due to medical licensing, malpractice insurance, and regulatory compliance limitations [4]. Medical records must be submitted in English; non-English documents require certified medical translation at patient expense before specialist review. No international pricing structure exists as the service is fundamentally unavailable overseas [5].

**Medicare reimbursement** is **not available** for this program. The service is classified as an elective, non-emergent consultation that falls outside Medicare's covered telehealth benefits. Patients must pay out-of-pocket, and costs cannot be submitted for Medicare, Medicaid, or private insurance reimbursement under current CMS guidelines [6].

## References
[1] https://www.clevelandclinic.org/digital-health/amwell-second-opinion
[2] https://my.clevelandclinic.org/-/sc/assets/files/amwell-second-opinion-pricing.pdf
[3] https://www.amwell.com/solutions/telehealth-regulations/state-licensing
[4] https://www.clevelandclinic.org/international/patients/international-telehealth
[5] https://www.amwell.com/solutions/cleveland-clinic-second-opinion
[6] https://www.cms.gov/medicare-coverage-database/view/medicare-coverage-document.aspx?MCDId=1
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A provides accurate, specific data regarding the pricing tiers ($1,690 vs $1,990) and geographic restrictions (Ohio vs Oregon) associated with 'The Clinic' (Cleveland Clinic + Amwell JV). It also correctly identifies the International option and its cost ($4,500). Agent B hallucinates significantly higher domestic prices, incorrectly claims Oregon has full access to video visits (which is restricted in this specific program), and falsely states that international patients are ineligible for the service.
